markdown:
## **German Expansion (1938-1939), Nazi-Soviet Pact, and the Outbreak of War - Events**


![German Expansion (1938-1939)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/df7b9dadbdf1e0bae8a82eccd25c8adf61070baa-1200x840.jpg)

## **Hitler’s seizure of the rest of Czechoslovakia (March 1939)**


1. Only six months after the Munich Agreement, **HItler started his expansion on the rest of Czechoslovakia and Lithuania.**
2. In March** **German troops marched into** the remaining Czech lands** (Bohemia and Moravia), turning them into a German protectorate. 
  1. In the same month, **he forced Lithuania to cede the Klaipėda (Memel) region. **
  2. The region had G**erman speaking inhabitants that had been cut off from Germany** because of the **Treaty of Versailles.**

****

![Hitler’s seizure of the rest of Czechoslovakia (March 1939)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/ae27ca07d2dd0504dea9aecc0e7f07d60bf8205e-1100x803.jpg)

## **Tension grow over Poland**


1. Between **October 1938 and January 1939, the German government put pressure on Poland over a series of territories**. 
  1. In particular, Germany was focused on **Danzig**, a city with a German speaking population that had become a “Free city” under League’s control.
2. Hitler also wanted free movement through the **Polish Corridor.** 
  1. The corridor gave Poland access to the Baltic Sea and separated East Prussia from the rest of mainland Germany.
3. **Poland resisted Hitler’s pressure** because they were confident in the support of France and Britain.
4. **Hitler did not think Britain and France would defend Poland,** but he was indeed **worried that they would secure a deal with Stalin** in order to protect Poland from German invasion.
5. To get ahead, **Hitler sent his Foreign Minister, Ribbentropp, to have secret meetings with his Soviet counterpart, Molotov.**

<callout type="note">Poland had a mutual defense** treaty with France that dated back to 1921,** and both **Britain and Poland had been vocal in favor of Poland **once Hitler betrayed the Munich Agreement.</callout>

****

![Danzig](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/024b22aac64b49c0edcebe5cd9a7aa2301883598-1200x798.jpg)

## **The Nazi Soviet Pact (August 23rd 1939)**


1. Germany and the Soviet Union pledged **not to attack each other** or support any third power that did, ensuring** peace between the two ideologically opposed states.**
2. **The Pact had a Hidden Clause: Eastern Europe would be divided into spheres of influence**, allowing the USSR to take **eastern Poland, the Baltic States (Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania), and parts of Romania**, while Germany would control **western Poland**.
3. **Only one week later, on September 1st, Hitler invaded (his side) of Poland and WW2 started.**

<callout type="warning">* **The Nazi Soviet Pact is not an alliance, but a non aggression pact!**
* **It assured Hitler that the USSR would remain neutral** and thus Germany would not have to fight a two front war.** **
* **For Stalin, it gave him time to rearm.**</callout>

## **The Betrayal of the Munich Agreement and the End of Appeasement**


1. When Hitler betrays the Munich Pact by taking the rest of Czechoslovakia, **Britain and France officially abandoned the policy of appeasement in favor of a “deterrence” strategy.**
2. Both Britain and France made **formal complaints **to HItler’s advances into Czechoslovakia and made **public declarations **of support for Poland.
  1. These promises were overall **symbolic and rather vague.** 
  2. As we have seen, **Hitler didn’t take them very seriously and continued to expand.**
3. **Britain did start some negotiations and talks with Stalin, but again in a very lukewarm style. **
  1. **Hitler got ahead of them and secured the Ribbentrop - Molotov pact for himself.**
4. After the Nazi Soviet Pact, **Britain formally allied with Poland** on August 25th, but when the attack started in September, **they did little to aid Poland.**
5. **Poland was attacked by Germany on September 1st and by the Soviets on the 17th, according to the secret clause of the Nazi Soviet Pact.** 
  1. Warsaw surrendered on September 27th with no real support from any ally.
6. **In late 1939, the Soviet Union pressured Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania into allowing Soviet troops on their soil. **
  1. Around the same time, a territorial dispute with **Finland** escalated into a brutal conflict known as the **Winter War, **in which the Red Army eventually forced territorial concessions after heavy losses.
7. **Stalin had expanded the USSR to Imperial Russia’s size.**
  1. By mid-1940, the USSR had expanded further by **fully incorporating** the Baltic states and seizing **Bessarabia** from Romania, **pushing its western border deeper into Eastern Europe.**

****

<callout type="self_review">* **Why did Hitler’s seizure of Czechoslovakia in March 1939 mark the end of Britain and France’s policy of appeasement?**
* **What strategic importance did the Polish Corridor and the city of Danzig hold for Germany, and why did Poland refuse Hitler’s demands?**
* **What were the key terms of the Nazi-Soviet Pact, and how did the secret clause shape the division of Eastern Europe?**
* **How did Britain and France respond to Hitler’s aggression after the Nazi-Soviet Pact, and why was their support for Poland limited in practice?**
* **How did Soviet expansion between 1939 and 1940 (including the Winter War and the annexation of the Baltic states) reflect the terms of the Nazi-Soviet Pact and Stalin’s ambitions?**</callout>
